TextENGNew: Say, for example, it's December—winter by the calendar.
What would you do if hot, muggy days with shrieking cicadas went on day after day?
Would you still faithfully go about your life in coat and muffler?
Of course not.
So even though it's June, after this many blazing hot days—as far as we're concerned, it's summer!
And if we're doing club activities in this heat, then a game like this is perfectly fair!
At our school, everyone does as they please in PE, but today was an exception—every classmate had assembled in the schoolyard.
Once she confirmed the full turnout, Mion raised her voice.
``All right, everyone—do we know what today is about?!``
changeReason: Old paraphrases as 'You ready?!' which loses the specific question about understanding today's purpose (主旨). New captures this but both are usable.

TextENGNew: "YEAHHHHH!!!!"
Every outstretched hand held a water gun.
After this many sweltering days, water guns in June are perfectly acceptable!
Besides, when the whole class joins in a firefight, getting excited is the only natural response!
"It does get one worked up, doesn't it—having club activities with the whole class!"
"It does! Getting to run around outside with everyone feels wonderfully fun, it does!"
"…It is fun for everyone else, but for those of us involved, we, as always, cannot afford to lose."
"Well, that's the shape of it! When there's anything called a competition, we club members are always carrying a certain risk."
"Hehehe! Exactly!"
"Club members, if you're careless enough to lose, there'll be quite a severe penalty waiting for you. Fight hard, each and every one!"
"Right, spirits are running high!! Attention, all combatants—are your weapons ready?! Magazines fully loaded?!"
"Leave it to us, Maebara-san! Water-gun combat is our greatest specialty!"
"…Tomita and the others play with water guns all the time, so they'll be formidable opponents."
"Hehehehe! Today we're a little different than usual!"
Tomita and Okamura exchanged confident grins—unusually assured for them—and raised their water guns with practiced ease.
You could see it in their faces: real skill had become real confidence, and that confidence had changed their very expressions!

"Mion goes without saying, but Satoko and Rika-chan both look like serious threats… Rena, what about you?! Try not to get picked off first by darting around too much!! Come to think of it, Rena's been a regular at the penalty game lately."
"Hau… W-well, there've been a lot of games I'm not good at lately… B-but I think Keiichi-kun has been just as frequent a penalty-game guest as I have."
"Hahahahaha!! Try walking home in your penalty-game getup and getting caught by your mother before you can change—then you can talk about pathos! Ughhh!!"
"W-well, last week's was just awful for me too—a normal girl might never be able to find a husband after that!!"
The two of us—recent regulars at the loser's end—shuddered in tandem remembering each other's spectacular punishments, and Satoko's high-pitched laugh rang out at our expense.
"Ohohoho! Aren't the two of you adorable? Well, if you don't want the next round of penalties, you'd better win, hadn't you?"
